title: ulex europaeus subsp. gallii
intro: Ulex europaeus subsp. gallii, also known as the gorse or furze, is a flowering shrub native to Europe, particularly in the western and northern regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Ulex europaeus subsp. gallii include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
